details.interpretation: KOSPI current bucket behaves as avoid/cautious boundary and active candidates did not survive scanner filters.
KOSDAQ current bucket was not rejected by market-policy avoid; blocks came from liquidity, hard-filter, and precision gating.

conclusion: Planner watchlist-only mode is working for learned avoid buckets without destroying upstream scanner logic.
KR policy split is behaving differently by market: KOSPI showed market-policy avoidance, while KOSDAQ remained governed by quality/risk gates instead of blanket policy rejection.
